---
title: "Activity 2"
format: pdf
editor: visual
author: 
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| warning: FALSE

#Add libraries as needed
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

#Read in Dataset
insurance <- read.csv("insurance_data_S26.csv")

```

priorclaims, sex, age, smoker, BMI

Backward BIC

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
##| label: bmi plot
#| echo: False

plot(insurance$charges, insurance$bmi,
     col  = adjustcolor("steelblue", alpha.f = 0.5),
     pch  = 19,
     xlab = "Charges",
     ylab = "BMI",
     main = "BMI vs Charges")

```

![](images/clipboard-3093701800.png)

If there is any correlation its very weal

```{r}
avg_charges <- tapply(insurance$charges, insurance$priorclaims, mean)

barplot(avg_charges,
        xlab = "Prior Claims",
        ylab = "Average Charges",
        main = "Average Charges by Prior Claims",
        col  = "lightblue")
```

Problem with this is that the \# of people with high prior claims is very low compared to people with 0-4 so we can't be certain that prior claims is a predictor

```{r}
boxplot(charges ~ sex,
        data = insurance,
        xlab = "Sex",
        ylab = "Charges",
        main = "Charges by Sex",
        col  = c("lightpink", "lightblue"))
```

Medians are identical so basically no relationship between sex and charges

```{r}
boxplot(charges ~ smoker,
        data = insurance,
        xlab = "Smoker",
        ylab = "Charges",
        main = "Charges by Smoker Status",
        col  = c("lightblue", "red"))
```

Looks like smokers do have a real difference in charges than non smokers and could serve as a good predictor

```{r}
set.seed(735)
train_index <- sample(
  x = 1:nrow(insurance),
  size = floor(0.80 * nrow(insurance))
)

insuranceTrain <- insurance[train_index, ]
insuranceValidation <- insurance[-train_index, ]

```

```{r}
full_model <- lm(charges ~., data = insuranceTrain)
backward_AIC <- stats::step(
  full_model,
  direction = "backward",
  k = 2,
  trace = 0
)

summary(backward_AIC)
```

```{r}
predictions_1 <- predict(backward_AIC, newdata = insuranceValidation)

RMSE_backward_AIC <- sqrt(mean((insuranceValidation$charges - predictions_1)^2))
RMSE_backward_AIC
```

```{r}
Null_Model <- lm(
  charges ~ 1,
  data = insuranceTrain)
forward_BIC <- stats::step(object = Null_Model,
            scope = list(lower = Null_Model, upper = full_model),
            direction = "forward",
            k = log(nrow(insurance)),
            trace = 0)
summary(forward_BIC)
```

```{r}
predictions_2 <- predict(forward_BIC, newdata = insuranceValidation)

RMSE_Forward_BIC <- sqrt(mean((insuranceValidation$charges - predictions_2)^2))
RMSE_Forward_BIC
```

```{r}
RMSE_insurance_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Forward BIC", "Backward AIC"),
  RMSE = c(RMSE_Forward_BIC, RMSE_backward_AIC)
)
RMSE_insurance_table
```
